assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9.
The intragroup dividend has been recognised as an intercompany receivable, ie a recognised monetary item rather than only a forecast transaction.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities and components of such items attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model and specifically discusses when intragroup foreign currency exposures can qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The provided context includes the separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation, which is a distinct IFRS hedge accounting approach relevant to intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidation.
